events.name: ACCESS_ACL_DENIED

events.name: ACCESS_PER_REQUEST_AGENT_EVENT

events.name: ACCESS_POLICY_AGENT_EVENT

events.name: ACCESS_POLICY_COMPLETED

events.name: ACCESS_SAML_ASSERTION

events.name: ACCESS_SAML_SLO_RESP

events.name: ACCESS_SESSION_CLOSED

events.name: ACCESS_SESSION_STARTED

events.name: ADAPT_REQUEST_HEADERS

events.name: ADAPT_REQUEST_RESULT

events.name: ADAPT_RESPONSE_HEADERS

events.name: ADAPT_RESPONSE_RESULT
events.description: Raised after the internal virtual server returns the result of response modification.

events.name: ANTIFRAUD_ALERT
events.description: Fires when an antifraud alert is received or generated.

events.name: ANTIFRAUD_LOGIN
events.description: Fires on an antifraud login event.

events.name: ASM_REQUEST_BLOCKING
events.description: Fires when ASM is generating a blocking response (pre-LB). Allows modification of the reject page before it is sent. Fires in both Normal and Compatibility modes.

events.name: ASM_REQUEST_DONE
events.description: Fires after ASM finishes processing a request (pre-LB). Normal mode: fires after every request. Compatibility mode: does not fire (use ASM_REQUEST_VIOLATION).

events.name: ASM_REQUEST_VIOLATION
events.description: Fires when ASM detects a request policy violation (pre-LB). Compatibility mode: fires only when a violation occurs. Normal mode: violations reported via ASM_REQUEST_DONE instead. Deprecated event.

events.name: ASM_RESPONSE_LOGIN
events.description: Fires on an ASM response login event. Runs post-response.

events.name: ASM_RESPONSE_VIOLATION
events.description: Fires when ASM detects a response policy violation. Runs post-response (after HTTP_RESPONSE_DATA).

events.name: AUTH_ERROR
events.description: Triggered when an error occurs during authorization (deprecated; use AUTH_RESULT).

events.name: AUTH_FAILURE
events.description: Triggered when an unsuccessful authorization operation completes (deprecated; use AUTH_RESULT).

events.name: AUTH_RESULT
events.description: Replaces AUTH_SUCCESS, AUTH_FAILURE, AUTH_ERROR, and AUTH_WANTCREDENTIAL events.

events.name: AUTH_SUCCESS
events.description: Triggered when a successful authorization completes (deprecated; use AUTH_RESULT).

events.name: AUTH_WANTCREDENTIAL
events.description: Triggered when an authorization operation needs an additional credential (deprecated; use AUTH_RESULT).

events.name: AVR_CSPM_INJECTION
events.description: Triggered when the AVR profile is about to insert CSPM javascript into the response.

events.name: BOTDEFENSE_ACTION
events.description: Fires immediately before Bot Defense takes action on a transaction (block, challenge, allow).

events.name: BOTDEFENSE_REQUEST
events.description: Fires on an HTTP request after Bot Defense processing completes, before a blocking decision is made. Pipeline: runs after L7 DoS in the BIG-IP 14.1+ processing order.

events.name: CACHE_REQUEST
events.description: Fires when a cacheable request is received (pre-LB). Allows cache key manipulation before the cache lookup.

events.name: CACHE_RESPONSE
events.description: Fires when a cached response is about to be served directly (pre-LB cache hit). Bypasses LB_SELECTED and server-side events when the response is served from cache.

events.name: CACHE_UPDATE
events.description: Fires when a new cache entry is inserted or an expired object is refreshed (post-response).

events.name: CATEGORY_MATCHED
events.description: Triggered when a custom category match is found during URL filtering.

events.name: CLASSIFICATION_DETECTED
events.description: Triggered when a flow is classified.

events.name: CLIENTSSL_CLIENTCERT
events.description: Fires when a client certificate is received (mutual TLS only). Only fires when the ClientSSL profile requires or requests a client certificate.

events.name: CLIENTSSL_CLIENTHELLO
events.description: Fires when the client's SSL/TLS ClientHello message is received. Fires after CLIENT_ACCEPTED, before CLIENTSSL_HANDSHAKE. Allows SNI inspection and certificate selection.

events.name: CLIENTSSL_DATA
events.description: Fires when SSL data is received from the client while in collect state (requires SSL::collect).

events.name: CLIENTSSL_HANDSHAKE
events.description: Fires when the client-side SSL/TLS handshake completes. Fires once per connection. After this, HTTP events can fire.

events.name: CLIENTSSL_PASSTHROUGH
events.description: Fires when the ClientSSL profile receives plaintext (non-TLS) data and enters passthrough mode.

events.name: CLIENTSSL_SERVERHELLO_SEND
events.description: Fires when BIG-IP is about to send its ServerHello on the client-side connection. Allows cipher and protocol modification.

events.name: CLIENT_ACCEPTED
events.description: Fires when a client connection is accepted (TCP: after 3-way handshake; UDP: on the first datagram). On UDP virtual servers, packet data is available via UDP::payload in this event.

events.name: CLIENT_CLOSED
events.description: Fires when the client-side connection closes (TCP: FIN/RST; UDP: idle timeout). Fires once per connection.

events.name: CLIENT_DATA
events.description: Fires when client data is received while the connection is in collect state (requires TCP::collect or UDP::collect). On UDP virtual servers, fires on every datagram including the first.

events.name: CONNECTOR_OPEN
events.description: Triggered when the connector is about to raise the service connect.

events.name: DIAMETER_EGRESS
events.description: Triggered when the system is ready to send a DIAMETER message.

events.name: DIAMETER_INGRESS
events.description: Triggered when the system receives a DIAMETER message.

events.name: DIAMETER_RETRANSMISSION
events.description: Triggered when the system generates a retransmitted DIAMETER request or answer message.

events.name: DNS_REQUEST
events.description: Fires when a DNS query is received (pre-LB). On UDP virtual servers, this is the first event (no CLIENT_ACCEPTED). On TCP virtual servers, fires after CLIENT_ACCEPTED.

events.name: DNS_RESPONSE
events.description: Fires when a DNS response is ready to be sent to the client (post-LB).

events.name: ECA_REQUEST_ALLOWED
events.description: Triggered when the ECA plugin successfully authenticates and is about to forward the request.

events.name: ECA_REQUEST_DENIED
events.description: Triggered when the ECA plugin cannot verify user credentials.

events.name: EPI_NA_CHECK_HTTP_REQUEST
events.description: Internal event for Network Access and Endpoint Inspector client applications (requires APM).

events.name: FIX_HEADER
events.description: Triggered when the system finishes parsing a new FIX header.

events.name: FIX_MESSAGE
events.description: Triggered when the system finishes parsing a new FIX message.

events.name: FLOW_INIT

events.name: GENERICMESSAGE_EGRESS
events.description: Raised when a message is received from the proxy.

events.name: GENERICMESSAGE_INGRESS
events.description: Raised when a message is received by the generic message filter.

events.name: GTP_PRIME_EGRESS

events.name: GTP_PRIME_INGRESS
events.description: Triggered for GTP prime messages on the connection that accepted the message.

events.name: GTP_SIGNALLING_EGRESS

events.name: GTP_SIGNALLING_INGRESS
events.description: Triggered for any GTP signalling message (except G-PDU) on the accepting connection.

events.name: HTML_COMMENT_MATCHED
events.description: Raised when an HTML comment is encountered.

events.name: HTML_TAG_MATCHED
events.description: Raised when an HTML tag is encountered.

events.name: HTTP_CLASS_FAILED
events.description: Fires when an HTTP request does not match any HTTP class filter (deprecated; pre-LB classification).

events.name: HTTP_CLASS_SELECTED
events.description: Fires when an HTTP request matches an HTTP class filter (deprecated; pre-LB classification).

events.name: HTTP_DISABLED
events.description: Fires when HTTP processing is disabled on the connection (e.g. WebSocket upgrade or protocol switch).

events.name: HTTP_PROXY_CONNECT
events.description: Fires when proxy chaining via the HTTP_PROXY_CONNECT profile.

events.name: HTTP_PROXY_REQUEST
events.description: Fires when a virtual server is configured with explicit proxy mode.

events.name: HTTP_PROXY_RESPONSE
events.description: Fires when a response from the remote HTTP proxy is received.

events.name: HTTP_REJECT
events.description: Fires when HTTP encounters a parsing error and aborts the connection.

events.name: HTTP_REQUEST
events.description: Fires when request headers are fully parsed (pre-LB). On keep-alive connections, fires once per HTTP transaction. Pipeline: L7 iRules layer.

events.name: HTTP_REQUEST_DATA
events.description: Fires when HTTP::collect has gathered the specified amount of request body data. Only fires if HTTP::collect was called in HTTP_REQUEST.

events.name: HTTP_REQUEST_RELEASE
events.description: Fires when HTTP data is about to be released on the server-side. Last chance to modify request data before it reaches the server.

events.name: HTTP_REQUEST_SEND
events.description: Fires immediately before the request is forwarded to the server-side TCP stack (post-LB, after SERVER_CONNECTED).

events.name: HTTP_RESPONSE
events.description: Fires when response status and headers are fully parsed from the server. Fires once per HTTP transaction on keep-alive.

events.name: HTTP_RESPONSE_CONTINUE
events.description: Fires when the system receives a 100 Continue interim response from the server.

events.name: HTTP_RESPONSE_DATA
events.description: Fires when HTTP::collect has gathered the specified amount of response body data. Only fires if HTTP::collect was called in HTTP_RESPONSE.

events.name: HTTP_RESPONSE_RELEASE
events.description: Fires when HTTP data is about to be released on the client-side. Last chance to modify response data before it reaches the client.

events.name: ICAP_REQUEST
events.description: Raised after an ICAP command has been created but before it is sent to an ICAP server.

events.name: ICAP_RESPONSE
events.description: Raised after an ICAP response has been processed but before the result is sent to the adaptation virtual server.

events.name: IN_DOSL7_ATTACK
events.description: Fires when the L7 DoS profile detects an attack condition (pre-LB). Pipeline: runs after CACHE events and before ASM request-side events in the BIG-IP 14.1+ processing order.

events.name: IP_GTM

events.name: IVS_ENTRY_RESPONSE

events.name: JSON_REQUEST

events.name: JSON_RESPONSE_MISSING

events.name: L7CHECK_CLIENT_DATA

events.name: L7CHECK_SERVER_DATA

events.name: LB_FAILED
events.description: Fires when the system fails to select a pool or pool member, or when a selected resource is unreachable. Alternative to LB_SELECTED at the same logical point.

events.name: LB_QUEUED
events.description: Fires when a connection limit is hit at the pool or pool member level. Alternative to LB_SELECTED.

events.name: LB_SELECTED
events.description: Fires when the system selects a pool member. On HTTP virtual servers, fires after HTTP_REQUEST (per-request). On UDP (9.4+), fires after CLIENT_ACCEPTED but before CLIENT_DATA.

events.name: MQTT_SERVER_INGRESS

events.name: SERVERSSL_DATA

events.name: SERVER_INIT
